channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Walz faces backlash after defending Obama-era mandate repealed by Trump: 'Massive tax penalty'
item.description: Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz defended the merits of an individual mandate for healthcare after being pressed by Sen. JD Vance during the vice presidential debate.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: In VP debate, Walz acknowledges he wasn’t in China during Tiananmen uprising
item.description: APM Reports, the investigations and documentaries unit of MPR News, first reported that Walz’s claim during a 2014 congressional hearing on the 25th anniversary of the massacre didn’t square up with media accounts in 1989.
item.dc:creator: Tom Scheck, Curtis Gilbert, Caspar von Au, and Forest Hunt, MPR News

item.title: Walz’s account of son’s brush with gun violence offers poignant moment in VP debate
item.description: “Look, I’ve got a 17-year-old and he witnessed a shooting at a community center playing volleyball,” Walz said during the debate, referencing his son Gus. “Those things don’t leave you.
item.dc:creator: Sam Stroozas, MPR News

item.title: Fact-check fight: CBS News cuts candidates' mics after JD Vance objects to a moderator's correction
item.description: CBS News briefly muted the debate microphones of the two vice presidential candidates after Republican JD Vance objected to a fact-check made by Margaret Brennan, one of the network's moderators.
item.dc:creator: David Bauder, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Vance called Harris a 'border czar' during the VP debate. What does that mean?
item.description: Republicans, including former President Donald Trump and his 2024 running mate, Senator J.D. Vance of Ohio, have increasingly referred to Vice President Kamala Harris as a failed "border czar.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Jackson, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Poll: Who won the Walz-Vance VP Debate?
item.description: (NEXSTAR) - Political scientists will tell you that vice presidential nominees rarely swing the election in favor of the candidate at the top of the ticket. But poll after poll shows this election cycle coming down to a percentage point across a handful of key swing states.
item.dc:creator: Bill Disbrow, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: GOP lawmakers, leaders react to Vance, Walz debate: 'Spitting cold, hard truth'
item.description: Republican law makers and top voices reacts as Vice presidential candidates JD Vance and Tim Walz sparred during their first and only debate Tuesday night in New York City.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What was the QR code on screen during the vice presidential debate?
item.description: CBS News is getting creative as it attempts to conduct Tuesday's Vice Presidential debate fairly and productively.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Are convicted felons allowed to vote in Illinois?
item.description: With just five weeks until the 2024 presidential election, most U.S. citizens over the age of 18 are eligible to vote as long as they are registered. However, for some individuals, the right to vote may be more complicated due to a criminal conviction.
item.dc:creator: Joey Schneider, WGN 9

item.title: GOP leaders press Biden administration to get tougher on Iran after missile attack
item.description: Republican leaders on Capitol Hill are calling on President Biden to get tougher on Iran in the wake of Tuesday’s missile attack on Israel, urging the administration to adopt a series of specific measures designed to cripple the military powers of Tehran and its proxies.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Trump takes lead over Harris in North Carolina Quinnipiac survey
item.description: Trump leads with 49 percent support, Harris earns 47 percent support, Green Party candidate Jill Stein gets 1 percent support and Justice For All Party candidate Cornel West gets 1 percent support in the Quinnipiac University poll conducted in late September and released Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: Trump blames Biden, Harris for Iran's attack on Israel: 'Very close to global catastrophe'
item.description: Former President Donald Trump slammed President Biden and Vice President Kamala Harris after Iran launched an unprecedented ballistic missiles attack on Israel.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: U.S. Fish and Wildlife signs Tamarac co-stewardship agreement with White Earth
item.description: The U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service and the White Earth Nation have signed a co-stewardship agreement for the Tamarac National Wildlife Refuge in northwestern Minnesota. The agreement will not change public access to the refuge, but will give tribal resource managers more input in refuge management.
item.dc:creator: Dan Gunderson, MPR News

item.title: Why Tuesday's vice presidential debate could matter more than history suggests
item.description: Tim Walz and JD Vance might have the chance to reshape the political landscape Tuesday in their first and only debate.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Republicans eager to keep House control are open to Trump’s SALT reversal
item.description: Republicans are open to raising the state and local tax (SALT) deduction cap that the party championed and put in place under former President Trump seven years ago as they aim to win total control of government in 2024.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: California sues hospital over emergency abortion care
item.description: California Attorney General Rob Bonta (D) this week announced a lawsuit against Providence Saint Joseph Medical Center, alleging the hospital refused to provide an abortion to a woman despite her pregnancy presenting an immediate threat to her life and no longer being viable.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Should moderators fact-check? Veteran broadcast debate host, journalism professor weigh in
item.description: MPR News host emeritus Gary Eichten and University of Minnesota associate professor and director of the Minnesota Journalism Center Benjamin Toff joined Morning Edition host Cathy Wurzer to talk about the positives and perils of live political debates.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer and Gracie Stockton, MPR News

item.title: Trump suggests ‘1 rough hour’ of policing would end retail crime
item.description: The former president called for law enforcement to be freed up to "do their job" without restrictions.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Kamala Harris presents case for rebuilding the middle class in Las Vegas campaign stop
item.description: Kamala Harris wrapped up her second visit to Las Vegas since she became the Democratic presidential candidate, focusing on multiple policy issues.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Peguero, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Here are the rules for the Vance-Walz debate
item.description: The debate, which will kick off at 9 p.m. EDT on Tuesday from the network’s New York office, will be moderated by CBS anchor Norah O’Donnell and "Face the Nation" host Margaret Brennan.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Prop. 36 faces intense review as North County residents gather for informative session
item.description: Voters have a lot to consider this upcoming election when it comes to Proposition 36, a statewide ballot measure that seeks a tougher approach on crime.
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Franco, Fox 5
